✓ PASSEDShowcase: API health check with rich Allure steps > health check flow > performs full health check with detailed steps16 steps1 attachments0.2s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: API health check with rich Allure steps.health check flow.performs full health check with detailed steps
API TestingHealth CheckService Health Verificationblockerapihealth
Verifies the test server is healthy by hitting the /health endpoint. Inspects status code, response headers, and body content. Demonstrates api-step with auto-attached response metadata.
Execution steps
  • ✓performs full health check with detailed steps
    • ✓Create API contextbase-url=http://localhost:46557
      • ✓Verify context is ready
        • ✓expect: (some? ctx)
      • ✓[API] GET /health
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:46557/health → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:46557/health
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 15
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:56 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "status": "ok"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:46557/health'
```
        Raw file
      • ✓Verify HTTP status 200status=200
        • ✓expect: (= 200 status)
      • ✓Verify response is OK
        • ✓expect: (true? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓Verify response URLurl=http://localhost:46557/health
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? url "/health")
      • ✓Verify Content-Type headercontent-type=application/json
        • ✓expect: (some? ct)
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? ct "application/json")
      • ✓Verify response bodybody-length=15
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? body "ok")
        Health Response
        {"status":"ok"}
        Raw file

✓ PASSEDShowcase: API hooks with logging and auth injection > hooks in action > demonstrates auth injection and response logging via hooks15 steps1 attachments0.1s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: API hooks with logging and auth injection.hooks in action.demonstrates auth injection and response logging via hooks
API TestingHooksAuth Injection & Loggingnormalapihooks
Shows how hooks intercept requests and responses. on-request injects an Authorization header. on-response logs status codes to an atom for verification.
Execution steps
  • ✓demonstrates auth injection and response logging via hooks
    • ✓Configure hookshooks=:on-request, :on-response
      Hook config
      {:on-request  "Injects Authorization header"
 :on-response "Logs method + status"}
    • ✓Execute API calls with hooks active
      • ✓[API] GET /health
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:41509/health → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
Authorization: Bearer test-token
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 15
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:58 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "status": "ok"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:41509/health' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer test-token'
```
        Raw file
      • ✓[API] POST /echo with JSON body
        HTTP
        ## POST http://localhost:41509/echo → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
Authorization: Bearer test-token
```

### Request Body
```json
{
  "action": "create"
}
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 59
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:58 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/echo",
  "body": {
    "action": "create"
  }
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:41509/echo' \
  -X POST \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer test-token' \
  -d '{
  "action": "create"
}'
```

```
Authorization: Bearer test-token
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 57
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:58 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo",
  "query": "page=1&limit=10"
}
```

```bash
curl 'http://localhost:41509/echo?page=1&limit=10' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer test-token'
```

✓ PASSEDShowcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file. > traced API calls (no browser page) > traces GET requests with query params6 steps1 attachments0.1s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file..traced API calls (no browser page).traces GET requests with query params
API TracingGET TracingQuery params in tracecriticalapitracing
Performs GET requests through (.request (.context pg)) (BrowserContext.request()). The Playwright trace captures the full request URL, headers, query params, response headers, and response body — all without opening a browser page.
Execution steps
  • ✓traces GET requests with query params
    • ✓[API] GET /echo with params
      HTTP
      ## GET http://localhost:39953/echo?user=alice&action=view&limit=50 → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:39953/echo?user=alice&action=view&limit=50
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 73
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:00 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo",
  "query": "user=alice&action=view&limit=50"
}
```

```bash
curl 'http://localhost:39953/echo?user=alice&action=view&limit=50'
```

✓ PASSEDShowcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file. > traced API calls (no browser page) > traces POST requests with JSON body6 steps1 attachments0.1s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file..traced API calls (no browser page).traces POST requests with JSON body
API TracingPOST TracingRequest body in tracecriticalapitracing
Sends a POST with a JSON body through (.request (.context pg)). The Playwright trace captures both the request body (postData) and the echoed response body as resource files in the trace ZIP.
Execution steps
  • ✓traces POST requests with JSON body
    • ✓[API] POST /echo — create user
      HTTP
      ## POST http://localhost:39953/echo → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
POST http://localhost:39953/echo
```

### Request Body
```json
{
  "name": "Bob",
  "email": "bob@example.org",
  "role": "admin"
}
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 95
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:00 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/echo",
  "body": {
    "name": "Bob",
    "email": "bob@example.org",
    "role": "admin"
  }
}
```

```bash
curl 'http://localhost:39953/echo' \
  -X POST \
  -d '{

  "email": "bob@example.org",
  "role": "admin"
```

✓ PASSEDShowcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file. > traced API calls (no browser page) > traces full CRUD cycle in a single trace11 steps1 attachments0.1s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: API-only tests with Playwright tracing — every request and response (including POST bodies) appears in the trace file..traced API calls (no browser page).traces full CRUD cycle in a single trace
API TracingCRUD TracingFull lifecycle in trace viewercriticalapitracing
Performs GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE through (.request (.context pg)). Every request and response appears in the Playwright trace file — open it in trace.playwright.dev to see the full HTTP timeline.
Execution steps
  • ✓traces full CRUD cycle in a single trace
    • ✓[API] POST /echo — create
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
      HTTP
      ## POST http://localhost:39953/echo → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
POST http://localhost:39953/echo
```

### Request Body
```json
{
  "name": "Eve",
  "action": "create"
}
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 72
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:00 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "path": "/echo",
  "body": {
    "name": "Eve",
    "action": "create"
  }
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:39953/echo' \
  -X POST \
  -d '{
  "name": "Eve",
  "action": "create"
}'
```
      Raw file
    • ✓[API] GET /echo — read
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
      HTTP
      ## GET http://localhost:39953/echo?id=1 → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:39953/echo?id=1
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 46
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:00 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo",
  "query": "id=1"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:39953/echo?id=1'
```
      Raw file
    • ✓[API] PUT /echo — update
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))

```json
    "name": "Eve Updated"
```

✓ PASSEDShowcase: Combined API + UI testing — fetch data via API, render in browser > API data → Browser rendering > fetches API data and verifies it renders in the browser19 steps1 attachments0.2s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Combined API + UI testing — fetch data via API, render in browser.API data → Browser rendering.fetches API data and verifies it renders in the browser
Mixed API + UIData FlowAPI → Browser Renderingcriticalapiuimixed
Fetches JSON data from the API test server, then injects it into a browser page as HTML and verifies the DOM rendering. Uses (.request (.context pg)) (from BrowserContext.request()) so API calls appear in the Playwright trace alongside browser interactions. Demonstrates combining api-step (HTTP calls) with ui-step (browser interactions with before/after screenshots).
Execution steps
  • ✓fetches API data and verifies it renders in the browser
    • ✓Phase 1: Fetch data via traced API
      • ✓[API] GET /echo — fetch user data
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:34593/echo?name=Alice&role=Engineer&team=Platform → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:34593/echo?name=Alice&role=Engineer&team=Platform
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 80
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:01 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo",
  "query": "name=Alice&role=Engineer&team=Platform"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:34593/echo?name=Alice&role=Engineer&team=Platform'
```
        Raw file
      • ✓Verify API responsestatus=200, body-length=80
        • ✓expect: (= 200 status)
      • ✓Phase 2: Render data in browser
        • ✓[UI] Load user profile page
        • ✓[UI] Verify page titletitle=User Profile
          • ✓expect: (= "User Profile" title)
        • ✓[UI] Verify heading
          • ✓expect: (= "User Profile" (locator/text-content h1))
        • ✓[UI] Verify user namename=Name: Alice
          • ✓expect: (str/includes? text "Alice")
        • ✓[UI] Verify user rolerole=Role: Engineer
          • ✓expect: (str/includes? text "Engineer")
        • ✓[UI] Verify teamteam=Team: Platform
          • ✓expect: (str/includes? text "Platform")
        • ✓[UI] Verify raw API response is embeddedraw-length=80
          • ✓expect: (pos? (count text))
Download zip
✓ PASSEDShowcase: Combined API + UI testing — fetch data via API, render in browser > API data → Browser rendering > performs API health check then verifies dashboard15 steps1 attachments0.2s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Combined API + UI testing — fetch data via API, render in browser.API data → Browser rendering.performs API health check then verifies dashboard
Mixed API + UIDashboardHealth → Dashboard Rendernormalapiuimixed
Checks API health via (.request (.context pg)) (traced), then renders a dashboard in the browser showing service status. API calls appear in the Playwright trace alongside browser actions.
Execution steps
  • ✓performs API health check then verifies dashboard
    • ✓Check API endpoints (traced)
      • ✓[API] Health check
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:34593/health → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:34593/health
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 15
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:01 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "status": "ok"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:34593/health'
```
        Raw file
      • ✓[API] Echo service
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:34593/echo → 200 OK

```
content-length: 31
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:01 GMT
```

```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo"
}
```

    • ✓Render status dashboard
      • ✓[UI] Load dashboard
      • ✓[UI] Verify dashboard title
        • ✓expect: (= "Service Dashboard" (page/title pg))
      • ✓[UI] Verify all services show 200service-count=3, service-0-status=200, service-1-status=200, service-2-status=200
        • ✓expect: (= 3 count)
        • ✓expect: (= "200" code-text)
        • ✓expect: (= "200" code-text)
        • ✓expect: (= "200" code-text)
      • ✓Final dashboard screenshot

✓ PASSEDShowcase: Deep response inspection with allure steps > response dissection > inspects every field of api-response->map22 steps1 attachments0.1s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Deep response inspection with allure steps.response dissection.inspects every field of api-response->map
API TestingResponse Inspectionresponse->mapnormalapiresponse
Converts an APIResponse to a Clojure map and verifies every field. Shows parameters and attachments for each response component.
Execution steps
  • ✓inspects every field of api-response->map
    • ✓[API] GET /echo with query params
      HTTP
      ## GET http://localhost:40327/echo?user=alice&action=view → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
GET http://localhost:40327/echo?user=alice&action=view
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 64
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:59 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "method": "GET",
  "path": "/echo",
  "query": "user=alice&action=view"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:40327/echo?user=alice&action=view'
```
      Raw file
    • ✓Convert to Clojure map
      • ✓Verify :statusstatus=200
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status m))
        • ✓expect: (integer? (:status m))
      • ✓Verify :status-textstatus-text=OK
        • ✓expect: (string? (:status-text m))
      • ✓Verify :urlurl=http://localhost:40327/echo?user=alice&action=view
        • ✓expect: (string? (:url m))
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? (:url m) "/echo")
      • ✓Verify :ok?ok?=true
        • ✓expect: (true? (:ok? m))
      • ✓Verify :headersheader-count=3
        • ✓expect: (map? (:headers m))
        • ✓expect: (contains? (:headers m) "content-type")
        Response headers
        {"date" "Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:59 GMT", "content-type" "application/json", "content-length" "64"}
      • ✓Verify :bodybody-length=64
        • ✓expect: (string? (:body m))
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? (:body m) "GET")
        Response body
        {"method":"GET","path":"/echo","query":"user=alice&action=view"}
        Raw file
      Full response map
      {:status 200, :status-text "OK", :url "http://localhost:40327/echo?user=alice&action=view", :ok? true, :headers {"date" "Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:59 GMT", "content-type" "application/json", "content-length" "64"}, :body "{\"method\":\"GET\",\"path\":\"/echo\",\"query\":\"user=alice&action=view\"}"}
    • ✓Headers array (preserves duplicates)header-array-count=3
      • ✓expect: (vector? arr)
      • ✓expect: (every? (fn* [p1__22081#] (and (contains? p1__22081# :name) (contains? p1__22081# :value))) arr)
      Headers array
      [{:name "Date", :value "Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:59 GMT"} {:name "Content-type", :value "application/json"} {:name "Content-length", :value "15"}]
Download zip

com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Fire-and-forget requests with request!.standalone requests.hits multiple endpoints without context management
API TestingStandalone Requestsrequest! conveniencenormalapirequest!
Demonstrates request! for quick one-off HTTP calls. No context creation/disposal needed — fully self-contained.
Execution steps
  • ✓hits multiple endpoints without context management
    • ✓GET health checkstatus=200, ok?=true
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status m))
      • ✓expect: (true? (:ok? m))
      Response
      {:status 200, :status-text "OK", :url "http://localhost:38373/health", :ok? true, :headers {"date" "Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:59 GMT", "content-type" "application/json", "content-length" "15"}, :body "{\"status\":\"ok\"}"}
    • ✓POST with JSON datastatus=200, method-echo=POST
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status m))
      • ✓expect: (str/includes? (:body m) "Bob")
      Response body
      {"method":"POST","path":"/echo","body":{"name":"Bob","role":"admin"}}
      Raw file
    • ✓PUT updatestatus=200
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status m))
      • ✓expect: (str/includes? (:body m) "PUT")
    • ✓DELETE resourcestatus=200
      • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status m))
      • ✓expect: (str/includes? (:body m) "DELETE")
    • ✓Handle error statusstatus=404, ok?=false
      • ✓expect: (= 404 (:status m))
      • ✓expect: (false? (:ok? m))

com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Full CRUD lifecycle with api-step.CRUD operations on /echo.executes full Create-Read-Update-Delete cycle
API TestingCRUD OperationsFull Lifecyclecriticalapicrud
Demonstrates a full CRUD lifecycle against the echo endpoint. Each operation is wrapped in api-step for automatic response metadata capture in the Allure report.
Execution steps
  • ✓executes full Create-Read-Update-Delete cycle
    • ✓CREATE — POST new resource
      • ✓[API] POST /echo — create user
        HTTP
        ## POST http://localhost:44095/echo → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
POST http://localhost:44095/echo
```

### Request Body

```json
{
  "name": "Alice",
  "email": "alice@example.org"
}
```

```
content-length: 84
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:56 GMT
```

```json
  "path": "/echo",
  "body": {
    "name": "Alice",
    "email": "alice@example.org"
  }
}
```

```bash
curl 'http://localhost:44095/echo' \
  -X POST \
  -d '{
  "name": "Alice",
  "email": "alice@example.org"
}'
```
        Raw file
      • ✓Verify creation succeededresponse-body={"method":"POST","path":"/echo","body":{"name":"Alice","email":"alice@example.org"}}
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? body "POST")
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? body "Alice")
    • ✓READ — GET resource
      • ✓[API] GET /echo — fetch user

com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Multi-context API + browser form submission.multi-domain API with form.uses multiple API contexts and submits a browser form
Mixed API + UIMulti-DomainAPI Contexts + Form Submitcriticalapiuimulti-domain
Uses (.request (.context pg)) with per-request headers to simulate multi-domain API access (all calls traced in Playwright), then renders results in a browser form. Shows the full spel API testing workflow.
Execution steps
  • ✓uses multiple API contexts and submits a browser form
    • ✓Fetch from multiple services (traced)
      • ✓[API] GET users /health
        HTTP
        ## GET http://localhost:38165/health → 200 OK

### Request Headers
```
X-Service: users
```

### Response Headers
```
content-length: 15
content-type: application/json
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:02:02 GMT
```

### Response Body
```json
{
  "status": "ok"
}
```

### cURL
```bash
curl 'http://localhost:38165/health' \
  -H 'X-Service: users'
```

✓ PASSEDShowcase: Retry with exponential backoff and hook tracking > retry flow > retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff16 steps1 attachments0.2s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Retry with exponential backoff and hook tracking.retry flow.retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff
API TestingRetryExponential Backoffcriticalapiretry
Simulates a flaky endpoint that returns 503 twice before succeeding. Uses retry with exponential backoff. Hooks track all attempts. The Allure report shows the full retry lifecycle.
Execution steps
  • ✓retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff
    • ✓Execute retry loopmax-attempts=5, backoff=exponential, initial-delay=10ms
      • ✓Attempt 1: GET /status/503attempt=1, status=503
      • ✓Attempt 2: GET /status/503attempt=2, status=503
      • ✓Attempt 3: GET /healthattempt=3, status=200
      • ✓Verify final result is successfulfinal-status=200, total-attempts=3
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status result))
        • ✓expect: (= 3 (clojure.core/deref counter))
        Final response
        {:status 200, :status-text "OK", :url "http://localhost:35601/health", :ok? true, :headers {"date" "Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:01:58 GMT", "content-type" "application/json", "content-length" "15"}, :body "{\"status\":\"ok\"}"}
      • ✓Verify retry historyretry-count=2
        • ✓expect: (= 2 (count (clojure.core/deref retry-log)))
        • ✓First retry metadataattempt=1, delay-ms=10
          • ✓expect: (= 1 (:attempt r1))
        • ✓Second retry metadataattempt=2, delay-ms=20
          • ✓expect: (= 2 (:attempt r2))
      • ✓Verify attempt historyattempt-statuses=[503 503 200]
        • ✓expect: (= [503 503 200] (mapv :status (clojure.core/deref attempt-log)))
        Full attempt log
        [{:method :get, :status 503} {:method :get, :status 503} {:method :get, :status 200}]
Download zip

✓ PASSEDExact output verification for :test format > produces exact output for example.org recording2 steps0.0s
com.blockether.spel.codegen-test.Exact output verification for :test format.produces exact output for example.org recording
Execution steps
  • ✓produces exact output for example.org recording
    • ✓expect: (= result (str ";; =============================================================================\n" ";; Auto-generated by spel codegen\n" ";; Source: Playwright JSONL recording\n" ";; =============================================================================\n" "\n" "(ns my-app.generated-test\n" " \"Auto-generated Playwright test.\"\n" " (:require\n" " [com.blockether.spel.assertions :as assert]\n" " [com.blockether.spel.core :as core]\n" " [com.blockether.spel.page :as page]\n" " [com.blockether.spel.roles :as role]\n" " [com.blockether.spel.allure :refer [defdescribe it expect]]))\n" "\n" "(defdescribe generated-test\n" " (it \"recorded test\"\n" " (core/with-testing-page {:headless false} [pg]\n" " ;; New page: pg\n" " (page/navigate pg \"https://example.org/\")\n" " (assert/contains-text (assert/assert-that (page/get-by-role pg role/heading)) \"Example Domain\")\n" " (core/close-page! pg))))\n"))

```json
}
```

```
content-length: 57
content-type: application/json
```

```json
  "path": "/echo",

  }
```

```bash
}'
```

com.blockether.spel / com.blockether.spel.options-test25 total25 passed
✓ PASSEDRegression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)3 steps0.0s
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON).handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)
Execution steps
  • ✓handles JSON containing cookie paths (the bug that motivated this split)
    • ✓expect: (= json-payload (read-field co "storageState"))
    • ✓expect: (nil? (read-field co "storageStatePath"))

Navigates to example.org and validates HTTP response status, headers, URL, and body content. This is the most basic smoke test — if this fails, the internet is down.

    • ✓Navigate to example.orgurl=https://example.org
      • ✓expect: (instance? Response resp)
      • ✓Verify HTTP status 200status=200
        • ✓expect: (= 200 status)
        • ✓⏵ [status] HTTP status: 200
      • ✓Verify response OK
        • ✓expect: (true? (net/response-ok? resp))

        • ✓expect: (.contains url "example.org")

      • ✓Verify content-type headercontent-type=text/html

        • ✓expect: (.contains ct "text/html")

      • ✓Verify response body is non-emptybody-length=528

Smoke Testsexample.orgNavigation Flowcriticalsmokenavigation
Tests the full navigation lifecycle: load page → click outbound link → verify new page → go back → verify original page → go forward → verify we're back on the target page.
Execution steps
  • ✓clicks the IANA link, navigates away, and returns via back/forward
    • ✓Navigate to example.org
      Step 1: example.org loaded
    • ✓Click the 'Learn more' link
    • ✓Verify navigated to IANAiana-url=https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains
      • ✓expect: (.contains url "iana.org")
      • ✓⏵ After click URL: https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains
      Step 2: IANA page
    • ✓Go back to example.orgback-url=https://example.org/
      • ✓expect: (.contains url "example.org")
      • ✓⏵ After back URL: https://example.org/
      Step 3: back to example.org
    • ✓Go forward to IANA againforward-url=https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains
      • ✓expect: (.contains url "iana.org")
      • ✓⏵ After forward URL: https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains
      Step 4: forward to IANA
Full stdout log
After click URL: https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains
After back URL: https://example.org/
After forward URL: https://www.iana.org/help/example-domains

Navigates to example.org and inspects the HTTP request/response chain: method, headers, timing, and body content.
Execution steps
  • ✓inspects request and response details for navigation
    • ✓Navigate and capture response
      • ✓expect: (instance? Response resp)
      • ✓Inspect request detailsrequest-method=GET, request-url=https://example.org/
        • ✓expect: (= "GET" method)
        • ✓expect: (.contains url "example.org")
        • ✓expect: (true? (net/request-is-navigation? req))
        • ✓⏵ Request: GET https://example.org/
      • ✓Inspect response headersheader-count=11

        content-encoding: gzip
server: cloudflare
age: 2634
content-type: text/html
allow: GET, HEAD
expires: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 23:05:30 GMT
cf-cache-status: HIT
cf-ray: 9e9bc89acb121b51-SJC
date: Thu, 09 Apr 2026 19:05:30 GMT
last-modified: Tue, 07 Apr 2026 22:07:08 GMT
cache-control: public, max-age=14400
      • ✓Inspect response bodybody-length=528
        • ✓expect: (pos? (count body))
        • ✓expect: (.contains body "Example Domain")
